title: wedelia acapulcensis var. parviceps
intro: Wedelia acapulcensis var. parviceps, also known as Mexican daisy or yellow daisy, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the sunflower family (Asteraceae) and is native to Mexico and Central America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities, which are attributed to its bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Wedelia acapulcensis var. parviceps include its ability to treat digestive issues, fever, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it can also be used topically as a poultice or salve to treat skin irritations and wounds.
